title: How Long Did Hunter-Gatherers Truly Live?

chunks.title: Life Expectancy of Hunter-Gatherers
chunks.summary: The discussion reveals that while hunter-gatherers experience high rates of maternal and infant mortality, which lowers their average life expectancy, those who survive past adolescence can have life expectancies comparable to modern societies. Their health spans are often better, displaying a notable resilience against chronic diseases that afflict more urbanized populations.

chunks.title: Comparison of Health Between Hunter-Gatherers and Western Societies
chunks.summary: The speaker emphasizes the stark contrast in health between hunter-gatherers and individuals in Western societies. While Westerners may live longer on average, they suffer from various chronic diseases and limitations in quality of life starting much earlier in adulthood.
